title: What the Patriots are saying about Super Bowl rematch with Seahawks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots to open 2026 season with a rematch against the Seahawks, whom they lost to in Super Bowl LX.
Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III has left the Seahawks for Kansas City, adding anticipation to the matchup.
Cornerback Marcus Jones and teammates aim to change last year's outcome with focus and preparation for Week One.

title: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ review: A light, low-stakes ‘Star Wars’ movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' film bridges the scale of Star Wars trilogies with the intimacy of the Disney+ series.
The movie features Pedro Pascal as Mando on missions for the New Republic, alongside CGI and puppet effects for Grogu.
Directed by Jon Favreau, the film is a lighthearted escapade with references to Star Wars lore and new CGI characters.

title: Patriots’ Craig Woodson is eager to build on strong rookie season ‘I’ve got to prove myself again’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Craig Woodson aims to improve after his rookie season, where he finished fourth on the Patriots in tackles.
Woodson became the first Patriots rookie to recover a fumble and make an interception in a playoff game.
Woodson transitioned to wearing jersey No. 4 after Kevin Byard joined the team and took No. 31.

title: 2 of the most welcoming towns in the U.S. are in New England, according to World Atlas
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Burlington, Vermont, and Bar Harbor, Maine, are featured as welcoming towns by World Atlas.
Burlington hosts vibrant community events like Summer in the City and Festival of Fools.
Bar Harbor offers events such as the Bar Harbor Music Festival and Dawnland Festival of Arts & Ideas.

title: Canadian man living in Saugus charged with illegally voting since 2008
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sunny Manhertz, a Canadian, is charged with illegally voting in U.S. federal elections since 2008.
Manhertz, a permanent U.S. resident since 1987, allegedly signed a voter registration form falsely claiming U.S. citizenship.
If convicted, Manhertz faces up to five years in prison and a $250,000 fine for casting fraudulent ballots.

title: Patriots WR Kyle Williams feels he’s ready for a ‘night and day’ jump in second NFL season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kyle Williams aims to improve in his second NFL season with the New England Patriots, focusing on chemistry with quarterback Drake Maye.
Williams, weighing between 191 and 193 pounds, is focusing on bulking up to enhance his play strength and overall performance.
Williams recorded 10 catches for 209 yards and three touchdowns in his rookie season, aiming for a more significant role in 2026.

title: ‘Takeovers’ at Hampton Beach in N.H. lead to multiple fights and arrests
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: New Hampshire's Hampton Beach saw multiple fights and 50 arrests during planned 'takeover' events, police reported.
Authorities, prepared with crowd control teams, handled incidents with State Police in tactical gear amidst beach takeovers.
Charges from the arrests included alcohol possession, rioting, DUI, and assault, with ongoing investigation for additional charges.

title: Amtrak is expanding Berkshire Flyer service this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Amtrak's Berkshire Flyer service from NYC to Pittsfield, Massachusetts, resumes on June 12 with expanded service.
A new second round trip allows Berkshires travelers to leave Fridays and return Sundays through September 19.
The Berkshire Flyer will stop at six New York stations and offers free WiFi, bicycle, and pet accommodations.

title: Cambridge City Council votes to end use of ShotSpotter technology
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Cambridge City Council voted to end the use of ShotSpotter devices within 90 days, after a 5-2 vote with two present.
Critics say ShotSpotter is unreliable and leads to over-policing, while supporters argue it provides crucial information on gunshots.
Contracts will be terminated and devices removed; the technology was funded by Department of Homeland Security grants.

title: Grand jury indicts Boston police officer on charge of manslaughter in fatal shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley indicted for manslaughter in the fatal shooting of Stephenson King Jr. in Roxbury.
O'Malley allegedly shot King during a carjacking response; bodycam footage indicates no officers were threatened.
The Massachusetts Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission suspended O'Malley post-arrest; he's on paid leave.
